title: Retire packaged defaults — pi-project ships no seed schemas or seed block files
context: pi-project today ships default schemas + default block files under packages/pi-project/defaults/{schemas,blocks}/, copied into newly-initialized projects by initProject (packages/pi-project/src/index.ts:215-236). The seeding pattern is a recurring source of architectural debt: stale seeded files cannot be updated by package upgrades. The original seedExamples copy-on-first-run pattern in pi-behavior-monitors had this exact failure mode and was retired in favor of live three-tier discovery (packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:325-352, doc-comment explicitly cites the retirement). Even pi-project's additive-only initProject creates the same divergence the moment a default ships an updated version: the file exists in the user's project from a prior install and never updates. The embedded note in FGAP-001's proposed_resolution surfaced this as 'decision pending' but the directional choice is not pending; the decision has been made and needs first-class reification so forward work does not continue down the already-decided-against path.
decision: pi-project does not ship default schemas or default block files. Users install desired schemas and starter blocks via an opt-in mechanism — concrete shape of the install mechanism is unspecified but the directional choice is final: no auto-seeding, no copy-on-init defaults shipped from the package. Bundled defaults are retired. This applies to all future schema and block kinds (config, relations, etc.) and to the existing default set under packages/pi-project/defaults/ once the opt-in mechanism replaces it.
consequences: issue-074's framing changes from 'fix seedExamples short-circuit' to 'the seeded artifacts must not exist; remove the source of the recurring class of issues'.

The substrate-arc subagent's recommended bundle 1 (Substrate seeding) is misframed: shipping config.schema.json + relations.schema.json + default config.json + default relations.json into packages/pi-project/defaults/ violates this decision. The POC's outputs ship as documentation/examples users copy, not as auto-seeded defaults.
packages/pi-project/defaults/ existing contents are grandfathered until the opt-in install mechanism is specified; no new content lands there.
initProject's default-copying loops at packages/pi-project/src/index.ts:215-236 are scheduled for retirement once the opt-in mechanism replaces them.

Future schema/block updates flow to users via the opt-in mechanism, not via package upgrades silently changing on-disk content. This eliminates the entire class of stale-seeded-file divergence issues at the source.
FGAP-001's proposed_resolution embedded note 'we might ship no defaults at all, and/or move defaults to a separate install step users enact if they want. Decision pending.' is superseded by this DEC.

references.label: Original embedded note in FGAP-001 proposed_resolution preserving user wording
pi-behavior-monitors live three-tier discovery (the retirement parallel)
